findings.description: SQL injection vulnerability due to string concatenation. User-controlled input directly included in SQL query.
findings.reasoning: (1) Data flow: username flows into SQL query as a string. (2) Trust boundary: username is user-supplied external data. (3) Input validation: no parameterized queries or escaping. (4) Verdict: SQL injection vulnerability.
findings.remediation: Refer to OWASP Top-10 and NIST CWE guidance for this vulnerability class.

findings.description: Missing authentication allows unauthorized access to the reset password functionality.
findings.reasoning: (1) Data flow: req.query.userId is used in adminService.resetPassword without authentication. (2) Trust boundary: userId is user-supplied external data. (3) Input validation: no authentication check. (4) Verdict: Missing authentication vulnerability.
findings.remediation: Refer to OWASP Top-10 and NIST CWE guidance for this vulnerability class.

findings.description: User-controlled input in file path allows directory traversal and potential XSS via direct response write.
findings.reasoning: (1) Data flow: user-supplied 'file' parameter directly influences the file path. (2) Trust boundary: file path is not properly validated or sanitized. (3) Input validation: no checks for directory traversal or XSS prevention. (4) Verdict: Path traversal and potential XSS vulnerability.
findings.remediation: Refer to OWASP Top-10 and NIST CWE guidance for this vulnerability class.

findings.description: User-controlled content flows directly into HTML response without sanitization, allowing XSS. Malicious script tags in 'comment' will execute in victim's browser.
findings.reasoning: (1) Data flow: comment flows directly into the HTML response. (2) Trust boundary: comment is user-supplied external data. (3) Input validation: no sanitization or escaping. (4) Verdict: Reflected XSS vulnerability.
findings.remediation: Refer to OWASP Top-10 and NIST CWE guidance for this vulnerability class.
